- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Analysis notebook — growing record of ReAct steps with provenance.
2	
3	Each step records what tool was used, what it found, and which evidence
4	IDs produced the result. build_synthesis_context() outputs markdown with
5	[CITE:ev_xxx] tokens that point to ORIGINAL sources.

17	
18	
19	@dataclass
20	class AnalysisStep:
21	    """One step in the ReAct loop."""
22	
23	    step_number: int
24	    reasoning: str
25	    tool_name: str
26	    result: ToolResult
27	    elapsed_seconds: float

30	class AnalysisNotebook:
31	    """Accumulates analysis steps and produces synthesis context with provenance."""
32	
33	    def __init__(self, query: str, evidence_ids: list[str]):
34	        self._query = query
35	        self._evidence_ids = evidence_ids
36	        self._steps: list[AnalysisStep] = []
37	        self._data_points: list[dict] = []
38	        self._started_at = time.monotonic()
39	
40	    def add_step(self, step: AnalysisStep) -> None:
41	        """Add a completed step and accumulate its data points."""
42	        self._steps.append(step)
43	        if step.result.success and step.result.data_points_produced:
44	            self._data_points.extend(step.result.data_points_produced)
45	
46	    @property

48	        return list(self._steps)
49	
50	    @property
51	    def data_points(self) -> list[dict]:
52	        return list(self._data_points)
53	
54	    @property
55	    def has_data(self) -> bool:
56	        return len(self._data_points) > 0
57	
58	    @property
59	    def step_count(self) -> int:
60	        return len(self._steps)
61	
62	    @property
63	    def successful_steps(self) -> int:
64	        return sum(1 for s in self._steps if s.result.success)
65	
66	    def get_all_source_evidence_ids(self) -> list[str]:
67	        """Collect all unique evidence IDs across all successful steps."""
68	        ids = []
69	        seen = set()
70	        for step in self._steps:
71	            if step.result.success:
72	                for eid in step.result.source_evidence_ids:
73	                    if eid not in seen:
74	                        ids.append(eid)
75	                        seen.add(eid)
76	        return ids
77	
78	    def build_synthesis_context(self) -> str:
79	        """Build markdown context with [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens for synthesis.
80	
81	        Priority rules:
82	        1. If interpret_results exists, lead with it (the analytical prose)
83	        2. Follow with extract_numeric_data (structured data for reference)
84	        3. Skip comparison_table and statistical_summary entirely —
85	           the interpretation already incorporates their findings, and
86	           raw pivot tables are unreadable noise for the section writer
87	
88	        This ensures the section writer sees ANALYSIS first, then
89	        supporting data — never raw stat dumps or sparse pivot tables.
90	        """
91	        if not self._steps:
92	            return ""
93	
94	        has_interpretation = any(
95	            s.tool_name == "interpret_results" and s.result.success
96	            for s in self._steps
97	        )
98	
99	        # Tools to SKIP when interpretation exists (it supersedes them)
100	        # PQ-4: Keep comparison_table — it provides supplementary
101	        # context the writer can use for structured comparisons
102	        skip_when_interpreted = {
103	            "statistical_summary", "query_evidence_sql",
104	        }
105	
106	        sections = []
107	
108	        # Pass 1: interpretation first (if it exists)
109	        if has_interpretation:

111	                if step.tool_name == "interpret_results" and step.result.success:
112	                    sections.append("### Research Analysis")

114	                    sections.append(step.result.markdown)
115	                    sections.append("")

117	
118	        # Pass 2: supporting raw data (extraction only, skip stats/tables)
119	        for step in self._steps:
120	            if not step.result.success:

122	            if step.tool_name == "interpret_results":

128	            tool_title = step.result.tool_name.replace("_", " ").title()

2	
3	These are the FIRST tests for ``OutlineAgent``. They lock two fixes:
4

6	    -- registered, ``requires_llm=True``, and ADVERTISED to the decide LLM -- failed 100% of
7	    calls with "No LLM client available". It now constructs an ``OpenRouterClient`` on the CODE
8	    model for tools in ``_CODEGEN_TOOLS`` and awaits ``close()`` on every exit path.
9

11	    ``add_unfillable`` (UNFILLED + disclosed), NOT ``add`` (PENDING). A PENDING "(unassigned)"
12	    todo is routed to ``search_more_evidence`` by decide rule 1, which burns real web fetches on
13	    an error string and can auto-assign an outline section literally titled "(unassigned)".
14	
15	NO NETWORK: ``OpenRouterClient`` is monkeypatched to a recording stub in every test that can
16	reach the dispatch; the tool executors are fakes registered onto the agent's own registry.

input.sign_off_reason: Two P1s remain open and CONFIRMED by my own independent probe: (1) a successful execute_python's computed value is unreachable by the decide LLM through every one of the four possible paths (summary_for_llm drops the payload; execute_python produces no data_points_produced; cp4 persists only tool_name/reasoning/success; build_synthesis_context/to_entries are never consumed in the outline lane); (2) each execute_python dispatch now spends real CODE-model tokens for output nothing can observe. FIX 1/FIX 2 are genuine and mutation-locked (7/7 reproduced by me, both mutations kill the right asserts), but un-bricking the tool without a result-feedback path converted a free failure into a paid one. Additionally the orchestrator spawn template passed literal 'undefined' as wheel/branch/focus — this gate round targeted the right code only by the tester's inference, not by specification.

sota.gap: The defining property of SOTA tool-use agents (ChatGPT code interpreter, Gemini, FS-Researcher) is that computed results FEED BACK into the agent's context to inform the next decision. This wheel's outline agent can now execute python (FIX 1 real) but is structurally blind to the result: summary_for_llm (analysis_notebook.py:142-161) emits only 'tool_name [OK] (2.3s) — reasoning[:60]', execute_python sets no data_points_produced (tool_registry.py:627-639), and the outline lane never calls build_synthesis_context/to_entries. Until the payload (markdown/statistics/insights) reaches the decide prompt at outline_agent.py:1182, every compute is a paid no-op and the agent will either re-call the tool or finish believing a number exists that it never saw. The gap-ledger discipline (UNFILLED routing, veto, disclosure) is genuinely ahead of the baselines on honesty-under-failure; the result-feedback loop is the concrete remaining gap, and it is one contained fix away.

root_causes.why: summary_for_llm() renders each step as only 'step_number. tool_name [status] (elapsed) — reasoning[:60]' and never touches step.result.markdown, step.result.statistics, or step.result.insights. Independently probed: a successful execute_python carrying $4,371,208.55 yields a summary where '4371208.55', 'npv_30yr', and every payload token are absent. No secondary path exists in the outline lane: execute_python's success ToolResult sets no data_points_produced (tool_registry.py:627-639) so notebook.data_points stays empty; cp4 persists only tool_name/reasoning[:200]/success (outline_agent.py:519-527); build_synthesis_context/to_entries have zero consumers under src/polaris_graph/outline/. The decide LLM at outline_agent.py:1162-1163/:1182 therefore cannot act on any computed value — a silent unreachable-result defect, undisclosed to the agent.

FIX 1 constructs a real OpenRouterClient(model=outliner_code_model()) per execute_python dispatch and the tool spends real CODE-model tokens (generate_and_execute_analysis), but its entire output is dropped by the mechanism above and is barred from the render lane by invariant. Un-bricking the tool converted a FREE silent failure into a PAID silent waste: every call costs money and yields nothing observable to the agent or the report. The fix itself is correct and mutation-locked; the defect is that it shipped without the result-feedback half.

root_causes.file: orchestrator spawn template (not in worktree; the string 'undefined' appears in my own and the tester's prompts as wheel name, branch, worktree, and focus)
root_causes.why: Variable substitution failed in the wheel-spawning template, so both tester and gate received literal 'undefined' for every identity field. This round tested the right code only because the tester recovered tooluse@efe5af3 + compose@0615bc5 from OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md and git worktree list. If a third wheel was intended, this entire round graded the wrong target — a process-integrity defect in the harness, not the product.

A bare grep -c '429' matches token-count lines ('429 reasoning tokens', '1429 reasoning tokens') as well as HTTP status codes: 59 raw hits vs 24 real 'HTTP 429 Too Many Requests'. Any rate-limit pressure metric built on the bare grep is inflated ~2.5x and would mis-drive backoff/pacing decisions.

root_causes.file: box contention: root PIDs 966951 (run_s5_i3.py, 1h42m, hammering the shared OpenRouter account) and 8846 (VLLM::EngineCore, 3d14h, 31735 MiB on GPU1)
root_causes.why: Both are root-owned and unkillable from uid 1000, blocking (a) an independent reproduction of the compose 2.06s->0.60s A/B (fix verified PRESENT at abstractive_writer.py:833/:912 with sync _draft_passes_wrapper at :661, but the timing claim remains the previous driver's, unverified) and (b) 31.7 GiB of GPU1 VRAM. Environment defect, not product code.
